---
title: "nitrix.sparse.mesh"
generated: true
signature-hash: 452b0dd6a73ead76
description: "Triangle-mesh sparse adjacency constructors."
listing:
  id: nitrix-sparse-mesh-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-sparse-mesh/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.sparse.mesh`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Triangle-mesh sparse adjacency constructors.

Supports two mesh families with the same [`Mesh`](nitrix-sparse-mesh/Mesh.qmd) representation:

- **Arbitrary triangulations**: [`Mesh`](nitrix-sparse-mesh/Mesh.qmd) from
  user-supplied tensors.  The k-ring adjacency, edge incidence,
  and cotangent Laplacian are all derived combinatorially from
  `faces`.
- **Icosphere**: [`icosphere`](nitrix-sparse-mesh/icosphere.qmd) builds the canonical
  spherical mesh via recursive subdivision of the icosahedron.
  The vertex count follows $10 \cdot 4^{n} + 2$: $n=0$
  gives 12 (the icosahedron), $n=7$ gives 163842 (FreeSurfer's
  "ico7").

Operators are returned as an [`ELL`](nitrix-sparse-ell/ELL.qmd) for
[`semiring_ell_matmul`](nitrix-semiring.qmd) compatibility.

The construction is host-side NumPy -- the BFS for k-ring
adjacency and the icosphere midpoint table are not natural in
pure JAX (they need dynamic-shape intermediates).  The resulting
[`ELL`](nitrix-sparse-ell/ELL.qmd) is plain JAX from there.

Use cases:

- **Cortical surface analysis**: FreeSurfer-style icospheres for
  cross-subject registration.  [`mesh_k_ring_adjacency`](nitrix-sparse-mesh/mesh_k_ring_adjacency.qmd) gives
  the neighbourhood structure for smoothing / atlas alignment.
- **Surface-based convolutional networks**: the k-ring adjacency
  is the natural support for `spherical_conv` (already in
  `nitrix.geometry.sphere`); this module gives the underlying
  ELL constructor.
- **Cotangent Laplacian**: the discrete Laplace-Beltrami operator
  for surface processing.  Used in spectral surface analysis,
  smoothing, harmonic-coordinate maps.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_vertices` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `3` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `n_faces` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `n_adjacent_pairs` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `2` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n_coarse` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `n_fine` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-sparse-mesh-symbols}
:::
